plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and meet mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI and QQQ to favor income and broad index.
Buy XLV to diversify into a new, stable sector.
Maintain a significant cash buffer by reducing overall exposure.
Focus on capital preservation with tighter stops.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets around 1.5% per month, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower drawdown. Risk is reduced but potential upside is also limited.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL, exiting positions below stop and reducing leverage.
Trim QQQI as flagged by the scanner, rebalancing income exposure.
Add to CRWD, pressing into a strong-performing AI/Software name.
Buy GOOGL to introduce a quality tech name from the scanner.
Maintain a moderate cash buffer and balanced sector exposure.
balanced.read: This balanced plan targets around 2.5% per month, focusing on risk-first growth. It reduces overall leverage and diversifies holdings while adding quality tech. Expect moderate drawdowns with a balanced risk-reward profile.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to exit underperforming leveraged positions.
Trim QQQI to reduce flagged holding and free up capital.
Add significantly to CRWD, concentrating in a high-performing AI/Software name.
Increase SMH exposure, leaning into the semiconductor sector.
Buy ANET, adding a high risk/reward setup from the scanner.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets around 4% per month, aiming for stretch growth. It concentrates in high-scoring setups and uses leverage, which amplifies both upside and drawdown. Be aware of higher volatility and the daily decay/rebalancing of leveraged ETFs; strict stop discipline is crucial.

plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and shift to income.
Sell SOXL and SPAL, eliminating all leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell SMH and CRWD to reduce high-beta sector and single-name risk.
Significantly add to QQQI, maximizing income ETF exposure.
Trim QQQ to further reallocate towards income-generating assets.
Prioritize low drawdown and reliable monthly distributions.
income.read: This income plan targets around 1% per month, aiming to park cash for yield. It heavily rotates into income/covered-call ETFs, significantly cutting leverage and high-beta names. While prioritizing low drawdown and distributions, NAV can still fall in a selloff, and distributions are variable.
